oracle_cursor_fix_note: renderRefBxfade now advances cursor past pre-window events; correctness fix, not tuning

click_root_cause_correction.corrected_attribution: Cmajor DLL oscillator artifact (phase accumulator truncation) at 440 Hz
click_root_cause_correction.note: The click is inherent to the Cmajor DLL output and cannot be fixed without modifying the DLL or relaxing S_CLICK, both of which are frozen.

C.R3_failure_cause: click_max = 0.2471 > S_CLICK = 0.05 (Cmajor DLL oscillator artifact at 440 Hz, NOT a swap-boundary transient)

D.note: maxerr_twin==0 proves: (1) reset() on fresh main-thread performer is a phase no-op, (2) oracle cursor fix is correct (2 events delivered), (3) path logic is identical across oracle and live paths

E.role: candidate SHIP fix — worker-thread B + W_WARM=1 warm-up normalization
E.R3_failure_cause: click_max = 0.2471 > S_CLICK = 0.05 (Cmajor DLL oscillator artifact at 440 Hz; present in DLL output regardless of orchestration)

cause_confirmation.evidence_C: Variant C (main-thread B, no warm-up): maxerr_B_xfade == 0.0. shapeA (worker-thread B, no warm-up): maxerr_B_xfade == 0.4996.
cause_confirmation.evidence_D: Variant D (both on main thread): maxerr_twin == 0.0. reset() on fresh main-thread performer is confirmed a phase no-op.
cause_confirmation.evidence_E: Variant E (worker-thread B + W_WARM=1 warm-up): maxerr_B_xfade == 0.0. Warm-up normalizes worker-thread phase to match main-thread creation.
cause_confirmation.falsification_test: If maxerr_B_xfade stayed ~0.4996 in C, thread-of-creation would be falsified. It dropped to 0.
cause_confirmation.conclusion: Thread-of-creation is the cause of the 0.4996 crossfade anti-phase. Warm-up normalization (advance W_WARM=1 into scratch -> reset() -> latch) is the production-viable fix.

final_failure_reason: R3 fails on click_max = 0.2471 > S_CLICK = 0.05. The click is a Cmajor DLL oscillator artifact at 440 Hz (phase accumulator truncation), present at sample 858228 (block 3352) in the pre-crossfade A-only region. It appears identically in REF_A and all variant outputs. The click is NOT a swap-boundary transient — the K_SWAP boundary is explicitly excluded from the measurement (its diff is only 0.052). All crossfade-specific gates (maxerr_B_xfade==0, maxerr_reload_A==0, maxerr_reload_B==0, mix_err<=1e-6) pass.
fallback_verbatim: Investment-freeze on the live-crossfade event-replay bridge. Fallback strategy: **per-block idempotent frequency re-statement** (re-send current input values every block after swap; state converges within one block period) instead of latch-replay + crossfade.
